entity_accuracy.notes: S.K. Enterprises and Suresh ji are correctly and confidently unified as a single supplier entity. Explicitly labelled as 'Suresh ji is the proprietor of S.K. Enterprises, standard Indian SME convention'. No hedging, no soft assumption flag. The entity label 'S.K. Enterprises / Suresh ji (supplier)' is clean and unambiguous. Correct Hinglish parsing of 'maal' and 'kal subah tak'.

cross_thread_correlation.notes: Single thread provided — cross-thread correlation cannot be tested. The agent correctly notes that client and all-staff threads would provide context on which order this delivery serves. Appropriate single-thread handling.

ambiguity_flagging.notes: Flags are operationally useful: generic item reference ('maal'), no client linkage, single-thread limitation. The ambiguous correlations section explicitly confirms 'S.K. Enterprises and Suresh ji are confidently the same entity' — correct application of Indian SME naming conventions. No over-flagging. Pre-emptive delivery confirmation subtask is a good implicit detection.

passes: S.K. Enterprises and Suresh ji confidently unified as single supplier entity — no unnecessary ambiguity flag
Explicitly states 'standard Indian SME convention' — demonstrates domain knowledge
Delivery timeline correctly parsed: 'kal subah tak' = 25 Mar morning
Pre-emptive delivery confirmation subtask correctly generated
No false ambiguity flag on the entity relationship
Operationally relevant flags: generic item, no client linkage
notes: Improvement over previous run (85 -> 93). The key change: the entity merge is treated as confident rather than flagged as needing human confirmation. The agent explicitly cites Indian SME naming conventions as the basis for the merge, which is the right domain knowledge application.
